can you explain this a bit ?

yes, I have explained this in the documentation as well. The reason being, that we cannot be sure if all or any descendents have been provided as part of the JSON payload, so the best option here is to get all the descendents from the anchor and compare them to the JSON payload. By doing so we can ensure if descendents were added, removed or updated.
For instance, if I select OMIT_DESCENDANTS then the anchor data will only contain the parent data nodes and let's say the JSON payload has all the descendents included. Then the delta report would show that some new descendents have been added. But in reality, the data is already there under the anchor, just omitted in this case. 
This can be considered as a discrepancy in the delta generated vs the data in the database.

Note: this is a restriction only of this API. The API for delta between anchors does provide the fetch descendents option

it is similar to buildDataNodes at line 346. This uses yang Resource instead of anchor name.
And if the xpath is root node then it parses json to data nodes and returns them else in other case it does the same thing but with a particular xpath and returns data nodes after parsing a json. So, it does the same thing that is parse json and return data nodes specific to a xpath where root node is a special case.
I see this being used in multiple places in existing code as well. Should I change them as well. We can include more detailed message informing under which xpath the nodes are not found
moved before
Ack

no, over here I am creating a data node with parent node name bookstore. I cannot explicitly create a data node with '/' as this would mean trying to create a data node without any parent nodes, basically meaning no data at all.
same as above

if you look into the FunctionalSpecBase.groovy.restoreBookstoreDataAnchor() when we setup the bookstore data, the data is modified to include the anchor number. Hence over here I am reading json as a string from the file and modifying it so it becomes similar to the data stored under the anchor during the setup process and then it returns a net zero delta because that would mean the data are same.
Another option would be to have a separate file with similar data or to insert the whole data a string.

we feel that the delate feature should NOT be in the data interface as it does NOT directly retrieve data!

@Arpit : if needed we can talk abt this.

Yes, I think we can discuss it over the daily scrum calls, but before that I will inform our stakeholders and get their suggestions. Also, it would be better to discuss and analyze if this change will impact their use cases beforehand.

Yep. Correct.
So just to summarize my understanding for you to put into context.

Now we can have just one endpoint ( a separate controller ) - if we look at Option#B above to cater our delta report requirement.

We can make the body flexible enough to decide which underlying method needs to invoked.

I wonder, do these getDelta methods belong in CpsDataService or CpsDeltaService?
My thinking is that methods in CpsDataService return DataNodes, but methods in CpsDeltaService return DeltaReports.

yes, the idea was to provide Delta feature as part of data service rather than its own separate service. Hence, we are using the Data Service to delegate our request to delta service.
I had a discussion with Toine and Priyank and we plan on having a single endpoint for delta with the possibility of having it as a separate service in a new patch. Just waiting for a response from stakeholders to identify any impacts on their use cases.
